exampleSentences: Em đăng kí lớp học tiếng Việt.
Anh đăng kí tài khoản mới.
Chị đăng kí sự kiện cuối tuần.
notes:
đăng kí (đăng ký) = to sign up / register for something.

It’s a verb you use when you put your name down to join, attend, or receive something. Very common with things like courses, exams, memberships, newsletters, competitions. You can also use it for online accounts and services.

Close synonym: ghi danh (more formal, often for schools or official lists). Both spellings kí and ký are correct; ký is more common in formal writing.
sentenceBreakdowns: Em (I) + đăng kí (sign up) + lớp học (class) + tiếng Việt (Vietnamese)
Anh (he) + đăng kí (sign up) + tài khoản (account) + mới (new)
Chị (she) + đăng kí (sign up) + sự kiện (event) + cuối tuần (weekend)
exampleSentencesTranslated: I sign up for a Vietnamese class.
He will sign up for a new account.
She will sign up for the weekend event.
notes_md:
**đăng kí (đăng ký)** = to sign up / register for something.

It’s a verb you use when you put your name down to join, attend, or receive something. Very common with things like courses, exams, memberships, newsletters, competitions. You can also use it for online accounts and services.


Close synonym: **ghi danh** (more formal, often for schools or official lists). Both spellings **kí** and **ký** are correct; **ký** is more common in formal writing.

example.vi: Tôi muốn đăng kí khóa học này.
example.en: I want to sign up for this course.

definitions.en: To record one's name officially in order to join or do something.
definitions.vi: Ghi tên vào một danh sách chính thức để tham gia hoặc thực hiện việc gì đó.

translation.en: Have you signed up for an account yet?
